scenario: Beacon Insurance routes 4000 claims per day through a Camunda 8.8 process. Each claim must follow exactly one outgoing branch chosen from the value of a routing variable, so the modeling team needs the BPMN gateway category that selects a single data-driven path.
question: Which gateway type routes the token along exactly one outgoing sequence flow based on data?

options.text: A parallel gateway routes the token by generating tokens on every outgoing sequence flow regardless of any data value attached to the flows
An exclusive gateway routes the token along exactly one outgoing sequence flow chosen by evaluating the data-driven conditions on the flows
An inclusive gateway routes the token along zero or many outgoing sequence flows depending on which condition expressions evaluate to true
An event-based gateway routes the token by waiting for whichever catching event reference receives an external trigger first in time

a.text: Incorrect. A parallel gateway generates new tokens on every outgoing path so several flows run concurrently, which is the opposite of selecting a single data-driven branch.

b.text: Correct. The exclusive gateway chooses one outgoing sequence flow out of many based on data, which matches the documented role of the BPMN exclusive gateway in Camunda 8.

c.text: Incorrect. An inclusive gateway may take several outgoing flows at once whenever multiple conditions hold, so it does not guarantee exactly one branch.

d.text: Incorrect. An event-based gateway routes on event arrival, not on a data condition, so the selected branch depends on external events rather than process data.

explanation: The documented gateways overview names the exclusive gateway as the element that chooses one sequence flow out of many based on data, in contrast to the parallel, inclusive, and event-based variants.
